methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

private.name: Inertia Enterprises
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Stardust Solutions
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Magrathea Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
DG Matrix is a Series A–stage energy and climate technology company founded by Haroon Inam and Subhashish Bhattacharya. The company has raised over $100M to date. The comparable universe consists of three private peers in the energy and climate sector (Inertia Enterprises, Stardust Solutions, and Magrathea Metals), with no directly comparable public companies available for benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression method applied to the company's $100M total capital raised as a proxy baseline. Using a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to later-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $200M. This single-method approach produces a triangulated range of $170M (low) to $230M (high), with a median of $200M.

The analysis supports a valuation centered near $200M, though with material constraints. The reliance on a single triangulation method and the absence of public market comparables or disclosed financial metrics limits the precision of this range. Key uncertainties include the underlying growth trajectory and unit economics of DG Matrix relative to the three private peers, as well as the applicability of the 2.0x multiplier to this specific stage transition and sector. Additional disclosure on revenue, growth rates, or market position would materially strengthen conviction in this valuation range.
